content.problemStatement:
You are given a binary string SSS of length NNN.

For a positive integer kkk, you can perform the following operation on string SSS:


Choose kkk elements from the string SSS;
Perform a total of kkk flips on the chosen kkk elements. A flip changes a `0` to `1` and vice versa. Note that you can use multiple flips on the same element.
Find the count of all such kkk in range [1,N][1,N][1,N], such that, you can apply the operation optimally, and make all characters of the string **equal**.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the length of the string SSS.
The next line contains a binary string SSS of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the count of all such kkk in range [1,N][1,N][1,N], such that you can apply the operation optimally and make all characters of the string **equal**.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Given S=111S = 111S=111, let us consider all values of kkk in range [1,3][1, 3][1,3]:

k=1k = 1k=1: It is impossible to make all characters of the string same after applying a flip.
k=2k = 2k=2: Choose S1,S3S_1, S_3S1‚Äč,S3‚Äč, and perform two flips on S1S_1S1‚Äč to obtain S=111S = 111S=111.
k=3k = 3k=3: Choose S1,S2,S3S_1, S_2, S_3S1‚Äč,S2‚Äč,S3‚Äč and perform one flip each to obtain the string S=000S = 000S=000.

Thus, we can make all characters of the string same, using k=2k=2k=2 and k=3k=3k=3.
Test case 222: Given S=1001S = 1001S=1001, let us consider all values of kkk in range [1,4][1, 4][1,4]:

k=1k = 1k=1: It is impossible to make all characters of the string same.
k=2k = 2k=2: Choose S2,S3S_2, S_3S2‚Äč,S3‚Äč, and perform one flip each on both to obtain S=1111S = 1111S=1111.
k=3k = 3k=3: It is impossible to make all characters of the string same.
k=4k = 4k=4: Choose S1,S2,S3,S4S_1, S_2, S_3, S_4S1‚Äč,S2‚Äč,S3‚Äč,S4‚Äč, and perform three flips on S2S_2S2‚Äč and one flip on S3S_3S3‚Äč to obtain S=1111S = 1111S=1111.

Thus, we can make all characters of the string same, with k=2k=2k=2 and k=4k=4k=4.
